MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Peter Soskin of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John Brosnan

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1 through 4 (negligent and strict liability design defect and failure to warn) and granted as to Issue 5 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony (as to the flange gaskets contained in the boxes with the Crane valves) and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the deposition testimony of William McLean and Anthony Pantaleoni create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (flange gaskets and internal bonnet gasket) attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his increased risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1 through 4 for the same reasons stated above. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to Issue 5 because defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to submit clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
